CLERK'S NTC OF FILING OF NTC OF APPEAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JULIO MESTRE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT DID REVIEW THE RECORD AND EXERCISED ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN CONCLUDING THAT THE DIRECTOR'S DECISION TO ACCEPT, IN PART, AND TO REJECT, IN PART, THE ARBITRATOR'S DECISION WAS SUPPORTED BY THE RECORD AND THE TERMS OF THE MOU. BOTH PARAGRAPHS 315 (TIME LIMITS FOR STEP 4) AND 316 (DIRECTOR'S DECISION) USE THE WORD "SHALL". THE MOU DOES NOT DIFFERENTIATE BETWEEN PROCEDURAL AND SUBSTANTIVE RIGHTS WHEN GRANTING THE DIRECTOR THE "DISCRETION IN ACCEPTING, MODIFYING OR REJECTING" THE ARBITRATOR'S DECISION AFTER A STEP 4 HEARING. THAT BEING SO, THE RECORD SUPPORTS THE DIRECTOR'S DECISION TO TERMINATE MR. MESTRE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
ORDER DENYING PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JULIO MESTRE'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment.
APPEARANCES: GREGORY ROTWEIN, ESQUIRE (415.397.0860) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MARGARET W. BAUMGARTNER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3859) APPEARING FOR RESPONDENT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The motion to vacate the judgment is denied. The Court did review the record and exercised its independent judgment in concluding that the Director's decision to accept, in part, and to reject, in part, the arbitrator's decision was supported by the record and the terms of the MOU. Both paragraphs 315 (time limits for Step 4) and 316 (Director's decision) use the word "shall". The MOU does not differentiate between procedural and substantive rights when granting the Director the "discretion in accepting, modifying or rejecting" the arbitrator's decision after a Step 4 hearing. That being so, the record supports the Director's decision to terminate Mr. Mestre. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
